data.title: Catalyst Watch: Spotlight on Nvidia, Alibaba, PCE inflation read, and the return of Bed Bath & Beyond
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Readers are invited to take part in Seeking Alpha's Weekly News Quiz 

 

Monday - August 25

  • Volatility watch - Opendoor (OPEN) and Asset Entities (ASST) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Avidia (AVBC), QMMM Holdings (QMMM), and Indivior (INDV). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Bragg Gaming Group Inc (BRAG), Monday.com (MNDY), and Vtex (VTEX).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include PDD Holdings (PDD).
  • IPO watch - No new IPOs are expected to be launched next week in the runup to the typical Labor Day slowdown. IPO quiet period expires on Figma (FIG), Ambiq Micro (AMBQ), and Avidia Bancorp (AVBC) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), S&P Global (SPGI), Hyatt (H), and Tyson Foods (TSN).
  • All week - The VMware Explore 2025 event will take place in Las Vegas. The VMware’s flagship technology conference under Broadcom (AVGO) ownership is one of the most influential enterprise IT gatherings of the year. A major highlight will be the keynote by Broadcom President and CEO Hock Tan on August 26, focusing on "Shaping the Future of Private Cloud and AI Innovation."
  • 7:15 p.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give a keynote talk before the Bank of Mexico Centennial Conference.

 

Tuesday - August 26

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Bank of Montreal (BMO), Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS), MongoDB (MDB), and Okta (OKTA).
  • All day - Starbucks (SBUX) officially brings back the wildly successful Pumpkin Spice Latte product along with the rest of the coffee chain's fall menu, which will also include seasonal favorites like the Pumpkin Cream Cold Brew, Iced Pumpkin Cream Chai, and Pecan Crunch Oatmilk Latte.
  • All day - Companies due to participate at the Jefferies Semiconductor, IT Hardware & Communication Technology Summit include Tower Semiconductor (TSEM), Amdocs (DOX), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), and ASML Holding (ASML).
  • 8:30 a.m. The July Durable Goods Orders will be released. Economists forecast a 1.2% year-over-year decline after the 9.3% decline in June.
  • 9:00 a.m. Economists have the release of the S&P/Case-Shiller House Price Index circled as a key update for the housing market.

 

Wednesday - August 27

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nvidia (NVDA), Royal Bank of Canada (RY), Crowdstrike (CRWD), Snowflake (SNOW), Veeva Systems (VEEV), Agilent (A), Pure Storage (PSTG), Five Below (FIVE), Williams-Sonoma (WSM), and HP (HPQ). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Kohl's (KSS) and Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - Companies due to participate at the two-day Deutsche Bank Technology Conference include Fortinet (FTNT), Cisco (CSCO), CoreWeave (CRWV), Arista Networks (ANET), Microsoft (MSFT), and ServiceNow (NOW).
  • All day - Rocket Lab Corp (RKLB), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), and Affirm Holdings (AFRM) will be added to the MSCI ACWI Index.
  • All day - The U.S. will begin to apply a 50% duty rate on certain Indian imports unless an extension or deal is worked out.
  • All day - Circana will release its July report on video game hardware and software sales.
  • All day - It is the five-year anniversary of XPeng's (XPEV) IPO.
  • 11:45 a.m. - Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak before the Greensboro Chamber of Commerce State of Our Community Luncheon 2025.
  • 2:00 p.m. - Nvidia (NVDA) will hold its earnings conference call. AMD (AMD) and Taiwan Semi (TSM) have the closest trading correlation to Nvidia. Suppliers that derive a significant portion of revenue from the chipmaker and typically trade directionally with it after earnings include Fabrinet (FN) and Micron (MU).

 

Thursday - August 28

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Dell Technologies (DELL), Marvell Technology (MRVL), Autodesk (ADSK), Dollar General (DG), Li Auto (LI), Best Buy (BBY) and Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Victoria's Secret (VSCO) and Affirm (AFRM) after their earnings are released.
  • 11:45 a.m. - Intel (INTC) CFO David Zinsner will participate in a fireside chat at the Deutsche Bank Technology Conference. It will be one of the first public appearances by Intel management since the company secured a $2B investment from Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) and learned that the U.S. government may acquire a stake.

 

Friday - August 29

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Alibaba (BABA).
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for July will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.8% year-over-year in June and 2.7% in May.
  • All day - The U.S. "de minimis" exemption, which has allowed most imports valued at $800 or less to enter duty-free, is set to end for nearly all countries.
  • All day - It is the last trading session before the first major weekend of the college football season that features several marquee match-ups, including Ohio State vs. Texas and Florida State vs. Alabama. The sports betting sector is expected to see an early surge of volume this year, which may increase investor interest in DraftKings (DKNG) and FanDuel owner Flutter Entertainment (FLUT).
  • All day - Beyond Inc. (BYON) will change its corporate name to Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY). The company currently owns Bed Bath & Beyond, Overstock, buybuy BABY, and other related brands and websites, as well as a blockchain asset portfolio.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story

data.title: Consumer Discretionary jumps 3% as Powell sparks rally: Here are 10 stocks to watch
data.description:
After lagging the broader market for much of 2025, the Consumer Discretionary sector came roaring back on Friday, climbing more than 3% to lead all S&P 500 (SP500) segments.

The rally followed upbeat investor reaction to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s latest comments at the Jackson Hole Economic Symposium. 

Against this backdrop, Seeking Alpha has highlighted the top 10 U.S. Consumer Discretionary stocks that stand out across three ratings systems — SA Quant Ratings, SA Analyst Ratings, and Wall Street Analyst Ratings — with each name carrying a Buy or Strong Buy designation. 

All companies on the list have market capitalizations above $1B, and the ranking is based on year-to-date performance: 

No. 1:National Vision Holdings (EYE), +130.7% YTD.

No. 2:GigaCloud Technology (GCT), +61.7% YTD.

No. 3: Stride (LRN), +61.4% YTD.

No. 4:Adtalem Global Education (ATGE), +46.2% YTD.

No. 5:Laureate Education (LAUR), +44.1% YTD.

No. 6: Hasbro (HAS), +42% YTD.

No. 7: Urban Outfitters (URBN), +34.6% YTD.

No. 8: Coursera (COUR), +32.7% YTD.

No. 9: BorgWarner Inc. (BWA), +31.6% YTD.

No. 10: Grand Canyon Education (LOPE), +25.3% YTD.

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

For further diversification towards the retail space, see below a grouping of exchange traded funds. 

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLY), (NYSEARCA:VCR), (NYSEARCA:FXD), (NYSEARCA:FDIS), (NYSEARCA:RSPD), and (NYSEARCA:RXI).

data.title: U.S. judge pauses changes to Affordable Care Act marketplace
data.description:
The U.S. District Court of Maryland on Friday issued a preliminary injunction halting the Trump administration from making changes to the Affordable Care Act's health insurance marketplace days before the changes were set to take effect.

U.S. District Judge Brendan Hurson in Baltimore sided with plaintiffs’ arguments that more than 2.2M could lose healthcare coverage due to the changes planned under the “Marketplace Integrity and Affordability” rule, which was scheduled for implementation on Aug. 25.

In the lawsuit filed by the legal group Democracy Forward on behalf of parties including the city of Chicago and Doctors for America in July, the judge paused changes as the case moves forward.

Hurson halted the implementation of almost all portions of the rule that plaintiffs challenged in court, including those related to higher fees and additional scrutiny targeted at low-income enrollees.

“By blocking the Trump-Vance administration’s dangerous rule, the court has protected millions of Americans from losing coverage, facing higher costs, and being denied essential benefits,” said Dr. Christine Petrin, Board President, Doctors for America.

Operators in the ACA marketplace include Centene (NYSE:CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Oscar Health (NYSE:OSCR), Elevance Health (ELV), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), and Cigna (CI).

data.title: AMD shares snapped six-session losing streak
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) shares inched up on Friday after six consecutive sessions of fall. The stock was up 2.4% at $167.64.

Chip stocks were in the green on Friday after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signaled the Fed will potentially cut rates in September if data warrants.

 

AMD’s shares lost 9.5% in the preceding six sessions. On a year-to-date basis, the semiconductor company advanced nearly 35%, compared to the broader market, which rose by 8.3%.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, AMD has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company has been graded A for profitability but is dragged down by a D+ grade for valuation. Wall Street analysts have issued a Buy call for the stock.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts have issued a Buy call for AMD with 24 analysts rating it Buy and above. Eight analysts suggest Hold while three recommend Sell.

"By broadening strategic partnerships with key hyperscalers and holding a nice pricing premium as long as it's possible for the right demand/supply equilibrium, I think that it's just inevitable that AMD will keep eating away at Nvidia's GPU market share over time," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: These consumer stocks ripped gains on interest rate relief
data.description:
Many stocks in the consumer sector sensitive to interest rates rallied on Friday after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell said conditions allow the FOMC to proceed carefully as it considers changes to the policy stance. "The baseline outlook and the shifting balance of risks may warrant adjusting our policy stance," he highlighted.

Investors may be looking at consumer spending relief tied to lower loan payments, as well as an improved outlook for corporate borrowing rates. 

Advancers included RH (RH) +11.0%, Leslies (LESL) +9.9%, MarineMax (HZO) +9.1%, Malibu Boats (MBUU) +8.4%, MasterBrand (MBC) +7.6%, Marriott Vacations (VAC) +7.5%, Winnebago (WGO) +7.0%, Brunswick (BC) +7.0%, Whirlpool (WHR) +6.6%, Caesars Entertainment (CZR) +6.2%, Carnival (CCL) +6.1%, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) +6.0%, and Signet Jewelers (SIG) +6.0%, and Tesla (TSA) +5.6%. 

Many of the stocks that ripped gains on Friday were playing catch-up from sluggish year-to-date performances.

data.title: ESPN in talks with MLB to strike a $1.65B three-year deal - WSJ
data.description:
Disney-owned sports network ESPN (NYSE:DIS) is reportedly in advanced discussions for a $1.65B three-year deal with Major League Baseball, according to The Wall Street Journal on Friday, citing sources.

Sources told WSJ that as part of the agreement, the baseball league's streaming service, MLB.TV, would be incorporated into ESPN's new direct-to-consumer offering.

The report said the deal would include some local, in-market rights and rights to carry MLB Network on the streaming service. ESPN would also get a national package of games available on linear and its streaming service.

The deal hasn't been finalized, and terms could change, sources told WSJ.

If the deal goes through, ESPN would pay the same amount it had under a previous agreement, which fell through earlier this year, as the sports network tried to renegotiate.

ESPN was due to pay the league $550M a year from 2026 to 2028 under the older pact for the right to air 30 regular season games per season, as well as the annual Home Run Derby and Wild Card playoff round, the report said.

On Thursday, WSJ reported that NBCUniversal (CMCSA) is inching closer towards a $600M three-year deal with MLB to carry games on NBC and the Peacock streaming service.

The Journal also reported that Netflix (NFLX) is trying to secure a deal to stream Home Run Derby for more than $35M a year, and that agreement would run through 2028.

data.title: 180 Degree shareholders approve merger with Mount Logan Capital
data.description:
180 Degree Capital (NASDAQ:TURN) announced on Friday shareholders' approval for the proposed all-stock merger with Mount Logan Capital (OTCPK:PYCFF).

The proposal garnered approval from 87.0% of the shareholders, while 12.3% voted against the business combination and 0.7% abstained from casting votes at the special meeting of shareholders held earlier this morning.

The two companies had amended the terms of their planned merger in response to feedback from shareholders.

The revised deal gives 180 Degree Capital shareholders a 10% premium to the company’s net asset value (NAV) at closing, up from the original 100% of NAV. This means 180 Degree Capital shareholders will receive a greater number of shares in the newly merged company, to be called "New Mount Logan".

data.title: Airline stocks outperform on Friday amid hopes for lower interest rates
data.description:
Airline stocks gained on Friday after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell said conditions allow the FOMC to proceed carefully as it consider changes to the policy stance. "The baseline outlook and the shifting balance of risks may warrant adjusting our policy stance," he highlighted.

Frontier Group (ULCC) saw the biggest gain in the sector, with a 12.5% jump. Other movers included American Airlines (AAL) +6.3%, Delta Air Lines (DAL) +5.9%, Southwest Airlines (LUV) +3.8%, United Airlines (UAL) +5.2%, JetBlue Airways (JBLU) +7.0%, Alaska Air Group (ALK) +6.8%, Allegiant Travel (ALGT) +9.4%, Mesa Airlines (MESA) +1.1%, SkyWest (SKYW) +4.2%, and Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) +8.5%.

After a tepid start to the year, U.S. domestic travel demand rebounded in July and August, with bookings up notably for late summer travel, offsetting earlier declines linked to international and business travel. However, overall air passenger growth has slowed compared to last year, running below 5% year-over-year.

Airfares in the U.S. broke a streak of declines with a 0.7% increase in July compared to a year ago on an unadjusted basis. On a month-to-month comparison, airfares were up 4.0% after falling 0.1% in June, declining 2.7% in May, and moving 2.8% lower in April, according to data compiled by the Bureau of Transportation Statistics.

For investors, airline stocks have been buoyed by largely positive Q2 earnings reports in the sector and a lower overall level of jet fuel prices.

data.title: Enphase Energy surges to top of S&P 500 after Powell appears to open path for rate cuts
data.description:
Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) pops to the top of the S&P 500 leaderboard Friday, +10%, after Federal Reserve Chair Powell hinted that policymakers may cut interest rates soon, which would benefit companies that depend on lower borrowing costs, including solar providers.

Among other top solar names: Fluence Energy (FLNC) +11.5%, Solaredge Technologies (SEDG) +11.2%, Shoals Technologies (SHLS) +9%, Sunrun (RUN) +8.1%, Eos Energy Enterprises (EOSE) +5.5%, Canadian Solar (CSIQ) +5.4%, Array Technologies (ARRY) +5.1%.

In his much-anticipated speech at the Fed’s annual symposium in Jackson Hole, Wyoming, Powell said "the balance of risks appear to be shifting" between the central bank's twin goals of full employment and stable prices, and "with policy in restrictive territory, the baseline outlook and the shifting balance of risks may warrant adjusting our policy stance."

First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) +4.8% as Guggenheim maintained its Buy recommendation and hiked its stock price target to $287 from $202, driven by its revised assumption that the company likely will continue operating its Malaysia and Vietnam facilities for the foreseeable future, albeit at lower utilization rates relative to its more advanced Series 7 capacity in the U.S. and India.

First Solar's (NASDAQ:FSLR) willingness to consider building a U.S.-based finishing facility to complete intermediate product from those two factories, and to claim the resulting $0.07/watt module credit, was "greater than we thought," Guggenheim's Joseph Osha wrote.

The analyst also expects the Treasury Department's pending clarification of FEOC rules to lend further support to First Solar's (FSLR) plans, and noted the tariff outlook for Malaysia and Vietnam appears to have stabilized at levels that make continued operations viable.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:TAN)
